opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My spine feels like it's fused solid with cement, the pain is burning through my lower back, and I absolutely cannot walk.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My axial psoriatic arthritis flared over two days — my lower back is locked and I can't walk; I'm on my own.

symptom_narrative.character: burning locked lower back/SI pain, near-total stiffness
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonising; I can't walk or bend
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: lower back and both buttocks (SI joints), not shooting down the legs
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: morning stiffness ~5 hours; pain better with movement, worse at rest
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: gentle movement eases it a little
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe fatigue; no saddle numbness or bladder/bowel changes
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have axial psoriatic arthritis and flare, but this is severe

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I live alone and can't safely stand; there are rugs between the bed and bathroom

communication_style.typical_message_style: describes inflammatory back pain clearly, in obvious pain

objective_data_offered.value: my lower back is locked; both buttocks/SI areas are very tender
I can still feel and control my bladder/bowels and the saddle area normally
